summary: Here’s a rough parameter-driven bracket concept that preserves the mounting geometry, adds drainage, and softens the stress risers a bit for fatigue resistance. I’ve kept it simple enough to hand-refine later rather than pretending this is a finished production part.
parameters: edge_margin = 10; // keeps holes centered on 30 mm plate
fit_checks: Hole spacing is preserved at 110 mm center-to-center.
Plate width stays at 30 mm, so the holes should remain centered with adequate side margin if hole_dia is kept near 9 mm or smaller.
Thickness is carried through as 8 mm.
Bend radius is noted as 12 mm, but the stub only mocks the bend shape for now; a proper bend-friendly solid will need hand refinement.
Drainage is included as a small hole away from the mounting holes to avoid trapping water.
Corners are softened with a 4 mm radius to reduce sharp transitions and help fatigue behavior.

One practical note: with only 30 mm plate width, you’ve got a fairly narrow strip for two holes. The geometry works in principle, but the edge distance is tight if the fasteners are large. If you want, I can tune this toward a more fabrication-realistic sheet-metal
export_detail: Exported CadQuery bracket assets as STL, STEP, and 3MF, then assembled a slicer pack for creality-k2-pro-combo using the fit-check-fast profile.
